title: Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update
overview:
An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Real Time (v. 7 ELS)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
kernel-rt-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.src.rpm
kernel-rt-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-debuginfo-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-devel-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-common-x86_64-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-devel-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-doc-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.noarch.rpm
kernel-rt-trace-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-trace-debuginfo-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-trace-devel-3.10.0-1160.145.1.rt56.1297.el7.x86_64.rpm

CVE-2023-53675: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

scsi: ses: Fix possible desc_ptr out-of-bounds accesses

Sanitize possible desc_ptr out-of-bounds accesses in
ses_enclosure_data_process().

CVE-2023-53705: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ipv6: Fix out-of-bounds access in ipv6_find_tlv()

optlen is fetched without checking whether there is more than one byte to parse.
It can lead to out-of-bounds access.

Found by InfoTeCS on behalf of Linux Verification Center
(linuxtesting.org) with SVACE.

CVE-2025-37823: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net_sched: hfsc: Fix a potential UAF in hfsc_dequeue() too

Similarly to the previous patch, we need to safe guard hfsc_dequeue()
too. But for this one, we don't have a reliable reproducer.

CVE-2025-68285: A use-after-free vulnerability was found in the Ceph client session initialization in the Linux kernel. The have_mon_and_osd_map() function checks map epochs without holding the appropriate locks, racing with concurrent map updates that free the old map. This can result in dereferencing freed memory during CephFS or RBD mount operations.
